entry.title: Change warehouse address details
entry.content:
Does anyone know where I can change the address details of a warehouse in customizing?

Thanks for your help in advance,
Hans Osendarp

entry.title: Re: No service provider was defined for CoCd XXXX
entry.content:
Hi @pablomunguia,

I understand that there is a missing Communication Arrangements app configuration. I would recommend reviewing the SAP KBA 3494135 - 'No service provider was defined for CoCd XX' error in eDocument Cockpit app

Hope this helps.

Arturo Montes de Oca

entry.title: Re: How to find reasons cloud usage GB - S/4HANA Cloud HA...
entry.content:
Hi Nitin,

Is there an expand option on that line? (looks like a caret on the left that could be expanded)

Could be lots of reasons for being over consumption - for example if there a lot of errors in the AIF logs (messages), it would need to be a really large number of errors but I have seen it.

There are several options you have to investigate.

You can check out the Technical Monitoring apps ( Table analysis for example: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0cc8af9d2f2e40f38b38b46b49325e2d/c26e285d90ce4f44ba20271805c1945b.html?locale=en-US) Technical Monitoring Cockpit (https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/index.html#/detail/Apps('F4031')/S33) (note this really a collection of apps)

SAP offers a number of Continuous Quality Checks to help you monitor the health of your system - one of them is the Data Volume Management service which gives you a report of which objects are consuming the most space/memory (https://support.sap.com/en/offerings-programs/enterprise-support/enterprise-support-academy/continuous-quality-check-improvement-services.html) This will help you and your customer decide on an archive strategy. (See Note https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3138230 about the CQCs - the note is a little out of date as it mentions solution manager but points you to the latest in SAP for Me)

Finally, the Data Volume Management topic on the community: https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/data-volume-management

Thank you

Jerry

entry.title: How to add output type button at delivery(vl03n) screen
entry.content:
Required same output type functionality like sales order(va03) in delivery (vl03n).

we want output type button on vl03n main screen like va03. we want to avoid extra>delivery output> header option.

Instead of that we want this option on main VL03n screen.

I have added the button but how to call the screen and please help with the enhancement point where i can call output type screen.

entry.title: Re: Incorrect path when activating an environment
entry.content:
Hi Patric, You might have run into the miniconda issue described here: https://github.com/ContinuumIO/anaconda-issues/issues/10595

Please try installing miniconda to a path without umlauts.

entry.title: Re: Launch Fiori Launchpad in the "Create a SAP Fiori based Table Maintenance app"
entry.content:
Hi @AnilKP,

the SAP BTP, ABAP environment comes with a built-in SAP Fiori Launchpad. To launch the built-in SAP Fiori Launchpad of the SAP BTP, ABAP environment trial instance, open the BTP cockpit, navigate to the ABAP service instance, and choose "View Dashboard". 

In the BTP ABAP trial, the Custom Business Configurations app is not exposed via a Fiori Launchpad Space as indicated in the screenshot in https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/abap-environment-maintain-bc-app.html#ab43b0eb-8fb7-4d7c-99a9-342591ffd7ae. However, you can search for the app and launch it.

Best regards
Kai

entry.title: GR not getting posted through BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE-code, but successfully posted by MIGO transaction
entry.content:
Hello experts

,

When I create a Goods Receipt with reference to Purchase Order using BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE, the following error message appears.

No goods receipt possible for purchase order <PO number> <line item number>.

But the GR is getting created manually using Transaction MIGO for the same PO number.

Here is the code:

SELECT deliverydocument,             "vbeln
             deliverydocumentitem,         "posnr
             plant                         "werks
             FROM i_deliverydocumentitem   "lips
             INTO TABLE @DATA(dlv_item)
             WHERE deliverydocument IN @inb_delv             " OR deliverydocument IN @delv_num )
             AND plant IN @Store.
      IF sy-subrc = 0.
        SELECT deliverydocument,          "vbeln
               supplier                   "lifnr
            FROM i_deliverydocument       "likp
            INTO TABLE @DATA(dlv_hdr)
            FOR ALL ENTRIES IN @dlv_item
            WHERE deliverydocument = @dlv_item-deliverydocument
            AND deliverydate IN @s_date_tmp
            AND sddocumentcategory = '7'.
        IF sy-subrc = 0.

          SELECT businesspartner,              "partner
                 bpidentificationtype,         "type
                 bpidentificationnumber        "idnumber
                 FROM i_bupaidentification     "but0id
                 INTO TABLE @DATA(bp_id)
                 FOR ALL ENTRIES IN @dlv_hdr
                 WHERE businesspartner = @dlv_hdr-supplier
                 AND   bpidentificationtype = 'GLREG'.
          IF sy-subrc = 0.

            SELECT low
              FROM ztle_constant
              INTO TABLE @DATA(legacy_vendor)
              WHERE name = 'ZS4_LEGACY_VENDOR'.
            IF sy-subrc = 0 AND legacy_vendor IS NOT INITIAL.

              LOOP AT bp_id ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<lfs_bp_id>).
*                READ TABLE legacy_vendor WITH KEY low = <lfs_bp_id>-bpidentificationnumber TRANSPORTING NO FIELDS.
*                IF sy-subrc = 0.
                IF line_exists( legacy_vendor[ low = <lfs_bp_id>-bpidentificationnumber ] ).
                  IF NOT dlv_hdr IS INITIAL.
                    DELETE dlv_hdr WHERE supplier = <lfs_bp_id>-businesspartner.
                  ELSE.
                    EXIT.
                  ENDIF.
                ENDIF.
              ENDLOOP.
            ENDIF.

            IF NOT dlv_hdr IS INITIAL.
              SELECT deliverydocument,                      "vbeln

                     deliverydocumentitem,                  "posnr
                     material,                              "matnr
                     plant,                                 "werks
                     actualdeliveryquantity,                "lfimg
                     baseunit,                              "Meins
                     referencesddocument,                   "vgbel
                     referencesddocumentitem,               "vgpos
                     goodsmovementstatus                    "wbsta
                     FROM i_deliverydocumentitem            "lips
                     INTO TABLE @DATA(dlv_item_final)
                     FOR ALL ENTRIES IN @dlv_hdr
                     WHERE deliverydocument = @dlv_hdr-deliverydocument
                     AND   ( goodsmovementstatus = 'A'
                     OR    goodsmovementstatus = 'B').
              IF sy-subrc = 0.
                SORT dlv_item_final BY referencesddocument referencesddocumentitem.



                SELECT b~purchasingdocument,                      "ebeln
                       b~purchasingdocumentitem,                  "ebelp
                       b~supplierconfirmationcontrolkey,          "bstae

                       a~purchasingdocumenttype                   "bsart

                       FROM i_purchasingdocumentitem AS b         "ekpo
                       INNER JOIN i_purchasingdocument AS a       "ekko
                       ON b~purchasingdocument = a~purchasingdocument

                       FOR ALL ENTRIES IN @dlv_item_final
                       WHERE b~purchasingdocument = @dlv_item_final-referencesddocument
                       AND   b~purchasingdocumentitem = @dlv_item_final-referencesddocumentitem+1(5)
                       AND   b~supplierconfirmationcontrolkey IN ( 'Z002' , 'Z003' )
                       AND   a~purchasingdocumenttype = 'ZDNB'
                       INTO TABLE @DATA(dsp_po).
                IF sy-subrc = 0.
                  SORT dsp_po BY purchasingdocument purchasingdocumentitem.


                  LOOP AT dlv_item_final ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<lfs_item>).
                    DATA(index) = sy-tabix.
*                    READ TABLE dsp_po WITH KEY purchasingdocument = <lfs_item>-referencesddocument
*                                               purchasingdocumentitem = <lfs_item>-referencesddocumentitem+1(5)
*                                               BINARY SEARCH
*                                               TRANSPORTING NO FIELDS.

*                    IF sy-subrc NE 0.
                    IF NOT line_exists( dsp_po[ purchasingdocument = <lfs_item>-referencesddocument
                                                purchasingdocumentitem = <lfs_item>-referencesddocumentitem+1(5) ] ).
                      DELETE dlv_item_final INDEX index.
                    ENDIF.
                  ENDLOOP.
                ENDIF.
              ELSE.
                MESSAGE 'GR is already done for this delivery'(007) TYPE 'I'.
                LEAVE LIST-PROCESSING.
              ENDIF.

              CLEAR: goodsmvt_item.
              SORT dlv_hdr BY deliverydocument.

              SORT dlv_item_final BY  deliverydocument deliverydocumentitem.


              LOOP AT dlv_hdr ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<lfs_dlv_hdr>).
                READ TABLE dlv_item_final ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<lfs_dlv_item>) WITH KEY deliverydocument = <lfs_dlv_hdr>-deliverydocument
                                                                                                             BINARY SEARCH.
                IF sy-subrc = 0.
                  DATA(item_indx) = sy-tabix.

                  goodsmvt_header-pstng_date = sy-datum.
                  goodsmvt_header-ref_doc_no = <lfs_dlv_item>-referencesddocument.
                  goodsmvt_code-gm_code      = gm_code_gr.

                  LOOP AT dlv_item_final ASSIGNING <lfs_item> FROM item_indx.
                    IF <lfs_item>-deliverydocument NE <lfs_dlv_hdr>-deliverydocument.
                      EXIT.
                    ENDIF.
                    APPEND VALUE #(            material             =  <lfs_item>-material
                                               plant                =  <lfs_item>-plant
                                               no_more_gr           =  abap_true
                                               mvt_ind              =  movement_indicator
                                               move_type            =  movement_type_gr
                                               entry_qnt            =  <lfs_item>-actualdeliveryquantity
                                               entry_uom            =  <lfs_item>-baseunit
                                               po_number            =  <lfs_item>-referencesddocument
                                               po_item              =  <lfs_item>-referencesddocumentitem+1(5)
                                               ref_doc              =  <lfs_item>-referencesddocument
                                               ref_doc_it           =  <lfs_item>-referencesddocumentitem+1(5)
                                               deliv_numb           =  <lfs_item>-deliverydocument
                                               deliv_item           =  <lfs_item>-deliverydocumentitem  ) TO goodsmvt_item.
                  ENDLOOP.

                  IF goodsmvt_item IS NOT INITIAL.
                    CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE'
                      EXPORTING
                        goodsmvt_header  = goodsmvt_header
                        goodsmvt_code    = goodsmvt_code
                      IMPORTING
                        materialdocument = materialdocument
                        matdocumentyear  = matdocumentyear
                      TABLES
                        goodsmvt_item    = goodsmvt_item
                        return           = return.

                    READ TABLE return WITH KEY type = 'E' TRANSPORTING NO FIELDS.
                    IF sy-subrc = 0.
                      CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_ROLLBACK'.
                    ELSE.
                      CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT'
                        EXPORTING
                          wait = abap_true.
                    ENDIF.

ENDIF.
                ENDIF.
              ENDLOOP.
            ENDIF.
          ENDIF.
        ENDIF.
      ENDIF.

entry.title: SAP Datasphere Sample Content for FI, HR and SD
entry.content:
It’s common to use predefined sample content to discover and master SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud. Previously, these samples required you to load data from CSV files. However, thanks to an innovative and recent improvement delivered in the Q4 2024 Quarterly Release Cycle updates, there is a new way to load the same data but with considerable benefits over its predecessor.

This article explains how to consume the sample data “SAP Datasphere Sample Content for FI, HR and SD,” in this new way and explains the benefits it provides. The sample uses the well-known Best Run Bike data and provides content for the Finance (FI), Human Resources (HR), and Sales (SD) lines of business.

Sample content

Sample content can be found in Semantic Onboarding for SAP Datasphere, while in SAP Analytics Cloud, it can be found in the Content Network. The same sample now appears twice in each of these locations as shown in these screenshots:

Here is the sample within SAP Datasphere – Semantic Onboarding:

Here is the sample in SAP Analytics Cloud – Content Network – Samples (once filtered to Sample Content for FI):

This means the sample has four variants, two for SAP Datasphere and two for SAP Analytics Cloud. But why are there now two of each?

While they support the same scenario, the way the sample data is loaded, and the availability of the sample content differs. The latest sample uses a new ‘Data Products’ solution and is shown with the name ‘SAP Datasphere Sample Content for FI, HR and SD’. It currently has version 1.0.0, unlike the now older sample with version 1.3.0 and 1.4.0. It may appear initially confusing that the latest version has an older version number, but this is because it’s using a new delivery platform called ‘Data Products’.

“SAP Sample Content for FI, HR and SD” (the older version) is available worldwide in all tenants, regardless of which landscape you are in (EU10, US10…). The sample data is uploaded via flat files into SAP Datasphere and this blog provides further instructions.

The new sample, “SAP Datasphere Sample Content for Fi, HR and SD” today with version 1.0.0, is currently only available for tenants in the EU10 landscape. It uses Data Products from SAP Marketplace to load the data, which means there is no need to create a connection or upload any CSV files. Thanks to Data Products, it’s now a direct out-of-the-box install, taking only a few clicks.

The remainder of this article guides you through the process of using Data Products to load the “SAP Datasphere Sample Content for Fi, HR and SD” into your SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytic Cloud tenants.


Activating sample content, a step-by-step guide, for "SAP Datasphere Sample Content for FI, HR, and SD" Version 1.0

Requirements

Assuming you are installing business content from SAP for the first time, you’ll need to create a Space for the content to be loaded into.

Create a new Space with Space ID “SAP_CONTENT” and name “SAP & Partner Content.” Grant the user who will import the content to this Space appropriate rights to create content.

After the Space is created with the ID mentioned, the content package can be imported. If the Space already exists, be sure to delete any objects that could conflict; otherwise, the new content will not be installed.

Download and install via Data Product

The out-of-the-box install is enabled by accessing Data Product via the SAP Marketplace.

1. In your SAP Datasphere tenant, open the Data Marketplace and search for the Data Product “SAP Datasphere Sample Content for FI, HR and SD - Sample Data”



2. Open the Data Product to see an overview and detailed descriptions. In the upper right corner, click ‘Load for Free’ to install the Data Product.

Please select the Space ‘SAP_Content’ as your target Space for which the Business Content will be installed:

3. In ‘Data Marketplace - My Data Products’ you’ll find an overview of all installed Data Products, their status and further details.

Activating business content via Semantic Onboarding
Once the Data Product has been successfully installed, you can start activating the sample content in both SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud.

Activating content in SAP Datasphere
In SAP Datasphere select ‘Semantic Onboarding’ and then ‘Content Network’ to show the ‘Samples’ tile:

Select the ‘Sample’ tile, which will then show two samples. Of these, select the “SAP Datasphere Sample Content” with version 1.0.0:

Select the ‘Deploy after import’ option and then ‘Import’:

You should then have three new Analytical Models in your Datasphere Space:

Sales Orders with Products   “SAP_SMPL_SALES_AM_SalesOrders”
Headcount                             “SAP_SMPL_HR_AM_EmpHeadcount”
Financial Transactions           “SAP_SMPL_FI_AM_FINTRANSACTIONS”

Previewing the Analytic Model “Sales Orders with Products” shows it's ready to be used and loaded with data:

The two other Analytical Models will currently show without data, as you need to populate them using several Data Flows.

Start these two Data Flows which can be found in the Data Builder. Open each and click the ‘Run’ icon (3rd icon from the left-hand side on the toolbar):

- Data Flow for HR   “SAP_SMPL_HR_DF”
- Data Flow for FI     “SAP_SMPL_FI_DF”

Once the run status is complete, preview the Analytical Model to verify the data has loaded successfully:

All necessary steps on the SAP Datasphere side are now complete and you can now activate content within SAP Analytics Cloud.

Activating content in SAP Analytics Cloud

For the SAP Datasphere content to be consumed in SAP Analytics Cloud you need to have a connection, with a particular name, to that SAP Datasphere tenant from SAP Analytics Cloud. Check your SAP Analytics Cloud tenant for a ‘SAPDWC’ connection and create a new one with this exact name, if you don’t have one:

Once you have a connection with the correct name, you may proceed and install the content in SAP Analytics Cloud.

Like SAP Datasphere, you’ll find the content in the ‘Content Network-Samples’. Use the filter to find ‘SAP Datasphere Sample Content for FI, HR an SD’


Open and click ‘Import’

Once successfully imported you can search for and use three stories that use the connection and consume the model held within SAP Datasphere.

SAP Analytics Cloud Story:

- Sales Orders (Sample Content SAP Datasphere)              “SAP_SMPL_DSP_Sales_Sample_Report”

- Headcount Reporting (Sample Content SAP Datasphere  “SAP_SMPL_DSP_HR_Sample_Report”

- Financial Overview (Sample Content SAP Datasphere)     “SAP_SMPL_DSP_FI_Sample_Report”

This concludes the step-by-step instructions for downloading, populating, and consuming models in Datasphere via Stories in SAP Analytics Cloud. It's all done with just a few clicks, and there's no more uploading CSV data files! All thanks to ‘Data Products’ introduced in the Q4 2024 Quarterly Release Cycle updates.

entry.title: Navigating Europe’s Energy Landscape: Key Themes and Strategic Priorities for a Sustainable future
entry.content:
1. Changes in European Politics and Policy

Overview: Since the 2019 European elections, the political climate in Europe has shifted. A reduction in Green party representation, coupled with the rise of right-wing parties, has made it harder to reach consensus on climate and energy policies. However, the European People’s Party (EPP) remains a strong political force.

Expanded Insights:

  • The rise of right-wing factions has made it more challenging to push through progressive environmental reforms, leading to political gridlock in key areas like carbon taxation and renewable energy subsidies.
  • Political compromise around the Green Deal is increasingly difficult, with parties divided on how to balance economic growth with environmental sustainability. This division is critical as Europe aims to reduce its carbon footprint by 55% by 2030 under the Fit for 55 package.

Key Takeaways:

  • The evolving political dynamics complicate the realization of climate goals.
  • Negotiating between economic concerns and environmental goals is becoming more challenging.

2. Climate and Energy Leadership

Overview: Europe's climate leadership has seen the rise of influential figures like Teresa Ribera (Executive VP for Climate and Competition) and Dan Jørgensen (Energy Commissioner), who are steering key discussions around market design and state aid policies.

5 challenges to tripling renewable energy in Europe:

  1. Regulatory Uncertainty: There are over 400 interventionist measures in the electricity market that are undermining investments in necessary renewable and low-carbon infrastructure.
  2. Permitting and Connection Delays: The process of obtaining permits for construction can take up to eight years, even though only two years might be necessary, causing significant delays.
  3. Lack of Storage: Insufficient storage capacity leads to the curtailment of renewable energy production, which lowers projected revenues for developers.
  4. Insufficient Demand: Stagnating demand and reductions are preventing the expansion of renewable energy production.
  5. Rising Costs and Unavailability of Raw Materials: Increases in the price of minerals and metals, along with supply chain bottlenecks, are escalating the costs of renewable energy systems (RES) and delaying projects.

Expanded Insights:

  • Teresa Ribera is seen as a stalwart in climate policy, advocating for stronger market mechanisms to support the transition to green energy. She has been instrumental in shaping the debate around how state aid should support renewable energy projects while avoiding market distortions.
  • Dan Jørgensen's role focuses on accelerating Europe’s electrification plans, with a particular emphasis on small modular reactors (SMRs) as a solution to bridge the gap between traditional and renewable energy sources. SMRs are being positioned as a viable nuclear option, combining lower costs and safety advancements.

Key Takeaways:

  • Strong leadership is vital to maintaining momentum on climate initiatives, particularly as political opposition mounts.
  • Leaders like Ribera and Jørgensen will play critical roles in shaping Europe’s energy future through market reforms and strategic investments.

3. Green Deal and Competition Policy

Overview: The Green Deal aims to make Europe the first climate-neutral continent by 2050. To achieve this, competition policy is being adapted to support the Green Deal’s goals by facilitating state aid for clean energy projects and enhancing public-private partnerships.

Expanded Insights:

  • A key aspect of the Green Deal’s success lies in adapting competition policy to avoid monopolies in the clean energy space while encouraging innovation. The European Commission is working on frameworks to ensure fair competition while allowing state subsidies where necessary to scale up renewable energy initiatives.
  • Procurement processes are also being streamlined to reduce barriers for green projects, especially for small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) involved in renewable technologies.

Key Takeaways:

  • Aligning competition policies with environmental goals is critical to driving the Green Deal forward.
  • Improved procurement frameworks will ensure that green projects can scale efficiently.

4. Electrification and Energy Transition

Overview: Electrification represents a major opportunity for Europe to reduce its carbon footprint. The transition involves not just renewable energy but also improving grid resilience and energy storage capabilities. Additionally, there is a notable shift in nuclear policy with the inclusion of small modular reactors (SMRs).

Framework needed for DSO (Distribution System Operator) grid investment, categorized into five key areas:

Regulation:
Challenge: Current regulations are not designed for a major investment push.
Solution: Align investment costs and growing operational expenditures (OPEX) with regulatory reforms that promote capacity growth.

Finance:
Challenge: Funding mechanisms, tariffs, and financial tools are insufficient to support the necessary investments.
Solution: Ensure easily accessible funding, facilitate financing instruments, and update existing rules to accommodate new investment needs.

Permitting:
Challenge: Slow and inefficient permitting processes hamper grid development.
Solution: Streamline permitting with a "bundled" approach to integrate renewable energy sources (RES) and grid investments more efficiently.

Supply Chain:
Challenge: There are shortages of critical materials, with 100% more transformers and lines needed.
Solution: Upscale EU manufacturing, enhance supply chains for efficient procurement and delivery, and improve recycling.

Skills:
Challenge: A skilled workforce of over 2 million people is required.
Solution: Create and upskill workforces, focusing on digitalization and automation to meet future grid requirements.

 

Expanded Insights:

  • Electrification of the energy system is seen as one of the most effective ways to achieve decarbonization, particularly in sectors like transportation and heavy industry. However, this requires significant investments in grid modernization and energy storage solutions to cope with the intermittency of renewables like wind and solar power.
  • SMRs offer a new direction for nuclear energy policy. Unlike traditional large-scale reactors, SMRs provide more flexibility, lower upfront costs, and can be integrated into existing grid infrastructures more easily. This is part of a broader energy diversification strategy to reduce reliance on fossil fuels.

Key Takeaways:

  • Electrification is a key driver of Europe’s decarbonization strategy, but grid modernization is crucial to support this transition.
  • SMRs provide a flexible nuclear energy option, supporting a balanced energy mix.

5. Energy Prices and Market Design

Overview: Europe’s energy market has faced significant disruptions, with prices soaring due to supply shortages, geopolitical instability, and market volatility. Policymakers are exploring ways to redesign the energy market to stabilize prices without resorting to blunt policy instruments that could harm the market's long-term stability.

Expanded Insights:

  • The Russian invasion of Ukraine has caused a dramatic spike in energy prices across Europe, leading to calls for government intervention. However, the speaker cautions against over-reliance on price caps or subsidies, which could distort the market and discourage investment in clean energy.
  • The energy market design is being reexamined to address these issues, with a focus on long-term contracts (such as Contracts for Difference) that can provide stability for both suppliers and consumers while avoiding sudden price fluctuations.

Key Takeaways:

  • Europe needs a careful approach to market reform to avoid short-term fixes that may undermine long-term energy goals.
  • A balanced approach to market design is crucial for stabilizing prices while encouraging investment in renewables.

6. Business Cooperation: A Path to Success

Overview: Collaboration between businesses, utilities, and governments has been essential in advancing Europe’s energy goals. Recent successes, such as the Power Summit and mobility initiatives, highlight the potential for these partnerships to drive forward the energy transition.

Expanded Insights:

  • Events like the Power Summit in Greece have demonstrated the power of cross-sector cooperation in driving innovation and investment in green energy solutions. These summits have also provided a platform for utilities, tech companies, and governments to share best practices and align their goals.
  • Business cooperation is seen as a fundamental component of the Green Deal's success. Public-private partnerships can help bridge the gap between governmental policy goals and the private sector’s ability to innovate and implement solutions at scale.

Key Takeaways:

  • Collaboration across sectors is vital to achieving Europe’s energy goals.
  • Public-private partnerships will be key to driving innovation and scaling renewable energy projects.

7. Strategic Vision: Energy Sovereignty and Industry Competitiveness

Overview: One of Europe’s top strategic priorities is achieving energy sovereignty by reducing its dependence on imported fossil fuels. However, this must be balanced with maintaining the competitiveness of European industries, which are facing rising energy costs.

Expanded Insights:

  • The current energy crisis has made energy sovereignty a top priority for Europe. By boosting domestic energy production through renewables and nuclear power, Europe can insulate itself from external shocks, such as supply chain disruptions and geopolitical tensions.
  • However, the challenge lies in ensuring that this transition does not undermine the competitiveness of industries that are energy-intensive, such as manufacturing and chemical production. Policymakers are working to provide subsidies and support mechanisms to help these industries transition to cleaner energy without losing their competitive edge.

Key Takeaways:

  • Energy sovereignty is essential to securing Europe’s energy future, but it must be achieved without compromising industrial competitiveness.
  • Support mechanisms will be needed to help industries transition to greener energy sources.

8. Investment in Grid Modernization

Overview: Europe's aging energy grids were not designed to accommodate the massive influx of renewable energy and electric vehicles that are expected by 2050. To meet future energy demands, significant investments are needed to modernize the grid, increase capacity, and integrate new technologies.

Expanded Insights:

  • A recent study has indicated that Europe needs to invest €67 billion annually to meet decarbonization and grid modernization targets. These investments will focus on expanding grid capacity, upgrading old infrastructure, and incorporating smart grid technologies.
  • Smart grids, which use digital technology to monitor and manage energy flows, will be critical for balancing the intermittent nature of renewable energy sources with fluctuating demand.

Key Takeaways:

  • Grid modernization is essential for integrating renewable energy and electric vehicles into Europe’s energy system.
  • Significant investment is required to upgrade infrastructure and develop smart grid technologies that can handle future energy needs.

Conclusion: A Collaborative Path Forward

Europe stands at the crossroads of an energy revolution, where the intersection of political challenges, market reforms, and technological advancements will determine its future trajectory. Achieving the goals of the Green Deal, ensuring energy sovereignty, and stabilizing energy prices will require a delicate balance between policy innovation and business collaboration. As the continent pushes towards electrification and decarbonization, strong leadership, strategic investment, and cooperative efforts will be crucial to building a sustainable and resilient energy future.

entry.title: Re: How to work with test cases and test plans in several rollouts?
entry.content:
Hello Ebba,

when it comes to your question regarding test plans creation, I would use the approach with Functional Area + Location (Rollout). 

Depending on the country / site where you plan rollout, you may have some process variations and therefore additional test cases and other testers, therefore I would recommend creation of test plans for each rollout and functional area.

As an example you may consider naming convention for your test plans:

  • TP IT FIN Wave 1 - UK
  • TP IT FIN Wave 2 - DE
  • TP IT Sales Wave 1 - UK.

Currently there is no test copy function available, however you can download the Excel list to see which test cases were included and use it while new test plan creation. 
Follow the SAP Cloud ALM Road Map for planned test plan copy functionality - https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?PRODUCT=73554900100800002513&range=CURRENT-LAST#;INNO=4B1ACEFEF2C91EDEB8A9571B8337A976

entry.title: How to get the get communication system id in multi tenant scenario?
entry.content:
Hi experts!

How do I get the correct System ID (comm_system_id) of an on-prem system in a multi-tenant scenario?

* --- get destination based on Communication Arrangement and the service ID
        DATA(lo_destination) = cl_http_destination_provider=>create_by_comm_arrangement(
          comm_scenario  = comm_parameter-comm_scenario
          comm_system_id = comm_system_id
          service_id     = comm_parameter-service_id ).

Thanks, Sebastian

entry.title: Seeking Guidance on Merging SAP CPI Integrations with API Management
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I hope this message finds you well. I'm currently working on a project  merge multiple SAP CPI integrations for different clients that have similar functionality. We aim to use SAP API Management to expose this merged integration while implementing specific requirements.

Here are the main objectives we are trying to achieve:

  1. Different Credentials for the Same API for Different Users: We want to ensure that different users can access the same API with unique credentials. What is the best approach to manage this? Should we create separate applications for each user, or is there a more efficient method, such as using OAuth-based authentication?

  2. Different Endpoints for the Same API: We aim to provide different endpoints for the same API based on the user's role or client ID. How can we configure API Management to handle multiple virtual paths while ensuring appropriate routing policies? What are the best practices for setting this up?

  3. Restricting Response Fields Based on the User: It's essential for us to restrict certain response fields based on the user accessing the API. What methods are available in SAP API Management to modify the response dynamically? Can we utilize JavaScript policies or transformation policies for this purpose?

  4. API Call Quota and Rate Limiting: Lastly, we need to enforce quotas on API calls to prevent abuse and manage usage effectively. What options do we have in API Management to implement these restrictions? How can we configure these quotas per user or application?

I would greatly appreciate any insights, best practices, or resources from the community to help us achieve these goals. Your expertise will be invaluable as we work to streamline our integrations and ensure robust API management.

Thank you in advance for your help!

Best regards,
Sathesh

entry.title: SCM - S/4 Hana Private Cloud Implementation - Course/Certificate guidance

Hi Everyone,
I hope you are all well. Thank you for taking the time to read my post. I am seeking your invaluable guidance.

I currently work as a System Optimization Analyst in the Supply Chain Department of the grocery retail industry, primarily working with ECC/FNR systems. Our organization is transitioning to S/4HANA Private Cloud in 2027. To support this transformation, I am looking to upskill by taking relevant courses and earning certifications.

I have started my learning journey on SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition course as a foundation and plan to pursue more specialized SCM courses afterward (Such as SAP Certified Associate - Production Planning and Manufacturing / SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP Business Network for Supply Chain) However, I am feeling uncertain about my chosen path and the learning journey towards certification.

  • The supply chain-specific course ( Production Planning and Manufacturing ) comes with a cost, whereas the learner journey for Private Cloud implementation is free with an SAP S-User ID. Is my approach correct, or am I missing something?
  • I have also begun the prerequisite courses for the certification. Are the contents provided in the learner journey sufficient for exam preparation?
  • Learning Journey on how to prepare for SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP Business Network for Supply Chain Shows only 4hr worth of content is it really sufficient for the assessment preparation.
  • Do any of these certificate act as prerequisite before pursuing specific SCM certificates. Taking into account that some few learnings are free and few have a cost associated either by paying for learning Hub or E-learning.

I'm looking for a holistic step-by-step approach. However, would appreciate focusing on key learning/certificates 1st in order to build my confidence and also display to my organisation/dept that the learning will benefit during our transformation phase.

Thanks in advance.

Bhargav Tadituri

entry.title: TWD decimal point issue
entry.content:
Dear Gurus,

Currency codes and decimal places are defined in the ISO-4217 standard. According to this standard, the Taiwanese Dollar (TWD) is expected to have two decimal places. However, the current configuration in the standard SAP TCURX table does not reflect this.

From a business perspective, the smallest denomination of coins in circulation in Taiwan is NT$1, as the Central Bank does not issue physical cents. Nevertheless, it is common practice to use "virtual" cents when determining unit prices and it is common to use "virtual" cents on invoices.

Is there a specific reason why the SAP standard does not align with ISO-4217 for TWD? What steps can be taken to address this issue?

Thank you and regards,

Dmitriy Listunov.

entry.title: How to Create and Edit Error Specific Messages in the Manage Substitution/Validation Apps
entry.content:
In this blog post, we'll explore how to create and edit error specific messages for validation rules, using your own parameters in the Manage Substitution/Validation apps. These apps are used across various sectors, including Joint Venture Accounting, Sales Accounting, Group Reporting, and more. I will also show you how to maintain custom error messages.

I will demonstrate how to do this using the Manage Substitution and Validation for Public Sector app (App ID: F7603). You will need the BPC Expert role assigned to your user.

First, let’s first look at how to create a rule.

Choose ‘Validation Rule’ as your rule type.

(Please note that error messages are only available for validations)

Select your Business Context and Event.

Choose ‘Create’ and fill out the ‘Preconditions’.

 

Maintaining custom error messages

You have the option of defining your own message class. This needs to be done upfront with developer extensibility. If you don’t have your own message class, you can use the standard message class FINRE_UI_STD. There, you can find a set of predefined messages with variables which need to be filled out by the customer.

The message 000, especially, allows a lot of flexibility in defining your own message text.

The parameters can be filled with constants, or field/functions.

Fill out the parameters with the variables you want to see in your error message, for example let’s use Cost Center for parameter 1, and Test for parameter 2, using constant.  

As you can see below, Cost Center and Test appear in your error message:

“Value for field Cost Center doesn’t comply with validation rule Test”.

 

Conclusion

Creating and editing validation rules with custom error messages in the Manage Substitution/Validation apps is a straightforward process that allows you to fine-tune your validations and error messages. By following these steps, you can ensure that your rules are executed according to your specific needs, making your workflow more efficient and user-friendly. Please let me know in the comments if you have any questions, I am happy to help.

Additional to the previous answer, which correctly described the most straightforward scenario by doing the usage decision and stock posting for the affected lot (via QA32, QA11, QA12), I can suggest to check following also:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/2bc3ee8d1c83404e8cf62418640004f2/ef84b855059b602de10000000a441470.html?locale=en-US
(nevertheless, it is not your option, if the lot was created by the goods receipt for the order)

- if the lot is stock relevant, and it is already closed, there might be some stock inconsistency related to it. Please check SAP Note 48815 and its report ZQEVAC20.

- last but not least, a useful KBA in the topic:
2656789 - Error CO440 while closing production order/process order

I wish you a nice day,

Albert
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Red/Yellow Flags of Stock Room Management Compatibili...
entry.content:
Hello @Shrikanth_P 

Just to be on the safe side, I would change the configuration to get to the green status, or at least to the yellow one. Assuming you are not using any of the questioned functionalities, it is safe and straightforward to do. You have issues in three areas:

Warehouse Control Unit

Go to SPRO Logistics Execution -> Warehouse Management -> Interfaces -> External Systems -> Configure Warehouse Management -> Function Modules for IDOC Creation and remove all the entries for the warehouse 115.

Wave Management

Go to SPRO Logistics Execution -> Warehouse Management -> Master Data -> Define Number Ranges -> Assignment (button Number Ranges) and remove a number range from the NR Group field for the warehouse number 115.

Cross Docking

Go to SM30, view T333_V and remove cross docking relevancy from all movement types for the warehouse number 115.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

For example, not sure if it is the same Payroll relief, but payroll is mentioned here in the service table: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0864cb07010642b3bde45a20de4975bc/795ae952dbbc494fbaae5ee7e57fac5a.html?locale=en-US

If these extra parameters are not standard fields, you will have to look into extensibility with one of the BAdIs 

Extensibility for Tax Calculation

Your tax calculation provider should be able to guide you on how those fields affect the tax calculation.

If you don't have the expertise already provided by your implementation partner, you should consider engaging SAP Consulting Services with an expert specialized in External Tax Calculation in Brazil.

Jerry

entry.title: Where can I start learning how to use SAP's generative AI Hub to solve your business problems?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Solving Your Business Problems Using Prompts and LLMs in SAP's Generative AI Hub learning journey

Overview
At the end of this learning journey, you will be able to use SAP's generative AI Hub to solve your business problems using LLMs, SAP AI Launchpad, and generative-ai-hub SDK. 
You will be able to apply techniques for refining and evaluating prompts using different large language models in generative AI hub.

Learning objectives

  • Describe AI foundation and generative AI hub 
  • Learn how to access SAP's generative AI hub.
  • Identify the need of using SDKs for LLMs.
  • Describe SAP's Generative AI hub SDK.
  • Explain  advanced prompting methods.
  • Describe different models in generative AI hub.
  • Configure different models in generative AI hub.
  • Explain generative AI applications beyond chatbots, using software-driven input and output processing scenarios.

Prerequisites

  • Navigating Large Language Models fundamentals and techniques for your use case

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Deploying Flux.1 Schnell Model on SAP AI Core
entry.content:
Flux.1 Schnell is a cutting edge Text2Image model from black-forest-labs capable of generating very realistic Images with a simple prompt, just tell what you need and you'll get it.

Here is an example, I gave the model the following prompt: Blue birthday cake with SAP logo on top

 

The Flux model can help you get the image that you are looking for in a couple of seconds/minutes, you can extend your prompt and get a result closer to what you expect.

The model as it is, is too big to be deployed on SAP AI Core, but we will quantize it to bypass this restriction. 

Before we start there are a couple of things that you need to prepare:

  1. I recommend you to start by doing the official SAP AI Core tutorials on this page 
  2. SAP AI Core access on SAP BTP
  3. Docker account connected to AI Core (you can find how to do it in the tutorials)
  4. Github account connected to AI Core (also follow the tutorial)

When you're done we can finally start, the best way to execute this code is with Jupyter Notebook:

Firstly let's connect to AI Core and create the resource group:

 

 

from ai_core_sdk.models import Artifact
from ai_core_sdk.ai_core_v2_client import AICoreV2Client

ai_core_client = AICoreV2Client(
    base_url = AICORE_API_BASE_JP,
    auth_url=  AICORE_AUTH_URL_JP,
    client_id = AICORE_CLIENT_ID_JP,
    client_secret = AICORE_CLIENT_SECRET_JP
)

response = ai_core_client.resource_groups.create("flux")
print(response)

 

 

Let's write the code to serve our model:

Firstly I create a Pipeline Manager to manage our model, since the Flux 1 Schnell model is too large, we quantize it to float16

 

 

import torch
from optimum.quanto import freeze, quantize, qint4
from diffusers import FlowMatchEulerDiscreteScheduler, AutoencoderKL
from diffusers.models.transformers.transformer_flux import FluxTransformer2DModel
from diffusers.pipelines.flux.pipeline_flux import FluxPipeline
from transformers import CLIPTextModel, CLIPTokenizer, T5EncoderModel, T5TokenizerFast

class PipelineManager:
    def __init__(self):
        self.pipeline = None
        self.load_model()  
        
    def load_model(self):
        dtype = torch.float16

        bfl_repo = "black-forest-labs/FLUX.1-schnell"
        revision = "refs/pr/1"

        scheduler = FlowMatchEulerDiscreteScheduler.from_pretrained(bfl_repo, subfolder="scheduler", revision=revision)
        text_encoder = CLIPTextModel.from_pretrained("openai/clip-vit-large-patch14", torch_dtype=dtype)
        tokenizer = CLIPTokenizer.from_pretrained("openai/clip-vit-large-patch14", torch_dtype=dtype)
        text_encoder_2 = T5EncoderModel.from_pretrained(bfl_repo, subfolder="text_encoder_2", torch_dtype=dtype, revision=revision)
        tokenizer_2 = T5TokenizerFast.from_pretrained(bfl_repo, subfolder="tokenizer_2", torch_dtype=dtype, revision=revision)
        vae = AutoencoderKL.from_pretrained(bfl_repo, subfolder="vae", torch_dtype=dtype, revision=revision)
        transformer = FluxTransformer2DModel.from_pretrained(bfl_repo, subfolder="transformer", torch_dtype=dtype, revision=revision)
        quantize(transformer, weights=qint4, exclude=["proj_out", "x_embedder", "norm_out", "context_embedder"])
        freeze(transformer)
        quantize(text_encoder_2, weights=qint4)
        freeze(text_encoder_2)

        DEVICE = "cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu"

        pipe = FluxPipeline(
            scheduler=scheduler,
            text_encoder=text_encoder,
            tokenizer=tokenizer,
            text_encoder_2=None,
            tokenizer_2=tokenizer_2,
            vae=vae,
            transformer=None,
        )
        pipe.text_encoder_2 = text_encoder_2
        pipe.transformer = transformer
        pipe.to(DEVICE)
        self.pipeline = pipe

    def get_pipeline(self):
        if self.pipeline is None:
            self.load_model()
        return self.pipeline

 

 

Than let's create a basic API using the fastapi to serve the model

 

 

from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException, UploadFile, File
from fastapi.responses import StreamingResponse
from io import BytesIO
import subprocess
import uvicorn
from pydantic import BaseModel
from typing import List
import asyncio
from asyncio import wait_for, TimeoutError
import os, logging, shutil, signal

app = FastAPI()

PIPELINE_MANAGER = PipelineManager()

# Define the inference request model
class TextToImageRequest(BaseModel):
    prompt: str
    guidance_scale: float =  0.0
    num_inference_steps: int =  4
    max_sequence_length: int =  256
    seed: int = 0

@app.post('/v1/generate-image')
async def generate_image(request: TextToImageRequest):
    try: 
        content = request
        prompt = content.prompt
        guidance_scale = content.guidance_scale
        num_inference_steps = content.num_inference_steps
        max_sequence_length = content.max_sequence_length
        seed = content.seed
        LOGGER.info("Request parsed")
        pipe = PIPELINE_MANAGER.get_pipeline()
        LOGGER.info("Pipeline received")

        # Generate the image
        generator = torch.Generator().manual_seed(seed)
        LOGGER.info("Torch generator created")
        image = pipe(
            prompt,
            guidance_scale=guidance_scale,
            num_inference_steps=num_inference_steps,
            max_sequence_length=max_sequence_length,
            generator=generator
        ).images[0]
        LOGGER.info("Image generated")

        # Save image to a BytesIO buffer
        buffer = BytesIO()
        image.save(buffer, format="PNG")
        LOGGER.info("Image saved")
        buffer.seek(0)

        # Send the buffer as a response
        LOGGER.info("Image is about to be returned")
        return StreamingResponse(buffer, media_type="image/png")
    except Exception as e:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=f"Error during the image generation. Details: {e}")

def main()->None:
    host_port = int(os.environ.get("HOST_PORT",8085))
    LOGGER.info("Starting application")
    uvicorn.run(app, host="0.0.0.0", port=host_port)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

 

 

Here are the requirements, save them as requirements.txt

 

 

transformers==4.43.4
transformer-utils==0.1.1
sentencepiece==0.2.0
torch==2.4.0
torchvision==0.19.0
urllib3==2.2.2
Werkzeug==3.0.3
protobuf==5.27.3 
optimum-quanto==0.2.4
accelerate==0.33.0
diffusers==0.30.0
fastapi==0.112.1
uvicorn==0.30.6
pydantic==2.8.2
typing==3.7.4.3
asyncio==3.4.3
requests==2.32.3
python-multipart==0.0.9

 

 

The next step is to create the docker Image, firstly log in in to your account:

 

 

docker login DOCKER_REGISTRY -u DOCKER_USERNAME

 

 

Than let's create our Docker file:

 

 

FROM pytorch/pytorch:2.3.0-cuda12.1-cudnn8-devel

WORKDIR /code

ENV HOST_PORT=8085
ENV PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF=expandable_segments:True
ENV HF_HOME=/code/cache
ENV HOME=/code
ENV PATH=/code/.local/bin:$PATH
ENV CUDA_HOME=/usr/local/cuda-12.1

RUN mkdir -p ${HF_HOME} && chown -R nobody:nogroup ${HF_HOME} /code

USER root

# Install build-essential (includes gcc/g++ compilers and related tools)
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y build-essential && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

USER nobody

COPY requirements.txt /code

RUN pip install --no-cache-dir --user -r requirements.txt

COPY main.py /code

USER root

RUN chown nobody:nogroup /code/main.py /code/requirements.txt 
RUN chown -R nobody:nogroup /code/main.py /code/requirements.txt 

EXPOSE 8085

CMD ["python", "main.py"]

 

 

Open again the terminal to create and push our docker image

 

 

docker build -t docker.io/DOCKER_USERNAME/flux:latest .
docker push docker.io/DOCKER_USERNAME/flux:latest

 

 

The next step is to create a GitHub repository and add the following serving yaml file to it:

 

 

apiVersion: ai.sap.com/v1alpha1
kind: ServingTemplate
metadata:
  name: flux-1 #Enter a unique name here
  annotations:
    scenarios.ai.sap.com/description: "Flux image generation"
    scenarios.ai.sap.com/name: "flux-clf"
    executables.ai.sap.com/description: "Flux Model Serving executable"
    executables.ai.sap.com/name: "flux-serve-exectuable"
  labels:
    scenarios.ai.sap.com/id: "flux-clf"
    ai.sap.com/version: "1.0.0"
spec:
  template:
    apiVersion: "serving.kubeflow.org/v1beta1"
    metadata:
      labels: |
        ai.sap.com/resourcePlan: infer.l
    spec: |
      predictor:
        imagePullSecrets:
          - name: your-docker-registry-secret
        containers:
          - name: kfserving-container
            image: "docker.io/DOCKER_USERNAME/flux:latest"
            ports:
              - containerPort: 8085
                protocol: TCP
            env:
              - name: HOST_PORT
                value: "8085"

 

 

It should look like this:

Remember to replace the DOCKER_USERNAME with your actual docker username and your-docker-registry-secret with your actual docker registry secret in AI Core.

We can now finally create an application in AI Core with the SDK, so open Jupyter once again:

 

 

response = ai_core_client.applications.create(
    application_name = "flux-clf-app-1",
    revision = "HEAD",
    repository_url = "https://github.tools.sap/GITHUB-USERNAME/flux-1", 
    path = "pipelines"
)
print(response)

 

 

If the operation was successful we can now switch to the AI Core GUI to complete the last steps, log in in to your account, choose the right workspace and resource group. Go to ML Operations -> Configurations -> Create

Choose the following parameters:

 Skip the Input Parameters and Artifacts and click "Create", now choose "Create Deployment"

After you create your deployment you have to wait a couple of minutes for the app to pull the model and start, when it's ready you will get the app url to use, it will look like this:

Now let's test our app:

 

 

import requests
from ai_core_sdk.ai_core_v2_client import AICoreV2Client
from dotenv import load_dotenv
import os
load_dotenv()

# Endpoint URL
base = "APP_URL"
url = base + "/v1/generate-image"

# JSON payload with parameters for image generation
payload = {
    "prompt": "A beautiful landscape of southern Germany, with some mountains and a lake",  
    "guidance_scale": 7,   
    "num_inference_steps": 50,  
    "max_sequence_length": 512, 
    "seed": 42                        
}

ai_core_client = AICoreV2Client(
    base_url = os.environ['AICORE_API_BASE_JP'],
    auth_url=  os.environ['AICORE_AUTH_URL_JP'],
    client_id = os.environ['AICORE_CLIENT_ID_JP'],
    client_secret = os.environ['AICORE_CLIENT_SECRET_JP']
)

headers = {"Authorization": ai_core_client.rest_client.get_token(),
           'ai-resource-group': "flux",
           "Content-Type": "application/json"}

response = requests.post(url, json=payload, headers=headers)

# Check if the request was successful
if response.status_code == 200:
    # Save the image to a file
    print("Image generated successfully")
    with open("generated_image.png", "wb") as f:
        f.write(response.content)
    print("Image successfully saved as 'generated_image.png'")
else:
    print(f"Failed to generate image. Status code: {response.status_code}")
    print(f"Response: {response.text}")

 

 

This is the result Image that we got back:

Here are some other examples of images generated with the Flux1 Schnell model:

 

 

 

Conclusion

The AI World is continuously evolving, a technology never changed this quickly before. Every new version provides a better result. In this race is crucially important to keep up with the evolution and SAP AI Core can help you with this.

Always use the AI ethically and responsibly .

Here are a couple of references:

- What is SAP AI Core

- SAP AI Core discover center

- Flux 1 Schnell official page

entry.title: Re: SAP Mobile Application for SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi JAGDISH98,

I believe you have your answer, you should access with the browser on your mobile device.

"As I am aware we can access the system using our Internet Mobile Applications like chrome, safari etc.,"

I am not sure what you mean by "with SAP Application" - do you mean like SAP GUI? (if so, the answer is no)

Not all apps are available on a mobile device. For example, if it is a legacy GUI/Harmonized UI app (like Create Sales Order - VA01), it does not run on a mobile browser. However, many Fiori - based apps are available to run on the mobile browser.

You can use the Fiori app library to determine if the app is designed to work on Desktop, Tablet or Mobile (Smartphone) device.

For example, 

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/index.html#/detail/Apps('F3069')/S33

Thank you

Jerry

entry.title: Re: SAP Activate Project manager Certification
entry.content:
Hi @MalgoMas, 
Our SAP expert @omar-sharaf  recommended to access the Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition learning journey where you also find the corresponding certification: SAP Certified Specialist - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition.
This certification exam validates that the candidate possesses the core knowledge required to support SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. It is designed for the Implementation Consultant & Project Manager who needs to prove that he/she has an overall understanding and in-depth skills of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and can participate in his/her role as a member of an implementation project. This is an entry level certification.
Please note: To prepare for this certification, it is necessary to take the Learning Journey Exploring SAP Cloud ERP in addition to the Learning Journey displayed under "How to Prepare."

However, casting a look at the underlying content Discovering SAP Activate - Implementation Tools and Methodology is essential for a better understanding of the learning journey above.

Kind regards
Margit

 

Thank you,

Omar

entry.title: [Sap Commerce cloud] basic authentication for SOAP webservices
entry.content:
We have some SOAP webservices and we want configure the basic authentication for these. The extention that contain all the resources is XYZ.

I added the XYZ-spring-security-config.xml into WEB-INF/config folder and I included into XYZ-web-spring.xml the tag import for read it. I'm sure that the security xml is correctly read because I put the '<debug />' tag and on the logs appear the warning log lines.

this is the configuration that I wrote for cover all the ws endpoints:

	<debug />
	<security:http pattern="/ws/**"
		use-expressions="true"
		authentication-manager-ref="wsAuthenticationManager">

		<security:http-basic />
		<security:intercept-url pattern="/ws/**"
			access="isAuthenticated()" method="POST" />
	</security:http>

	<security:authentication-manager
		id="wsAuthenticationManager" alias="wsAuthenticationManager">
		<security:authentication-provider>
			<security:user-service>
				<security:user name="admin" password="admin"
					authorities="ROLE_ADMIN" />
			</security:user-service>
		</security:authentication-provider>
	</security:authentication-manager>

If I broke the config xml (with wrong syntax or unknown/undefined tags) the web services doesn't work. If the xml is (apparently) correct the webservices works fine but the credential are ignored (and with correct, wrong or simply missing user-password) my application receive the message and return a 200 to client.

What wrong?

entry.title: SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding Transition, Guidance, and Lessons Learned from Previous Projects
entry.content:
In a recent webinar Onboarding Transition, Guidance, and Lessons Learned from Previous Projects, hosted on Tuesday, 1st October 2024, SAP SuccessFactors Consultants Angela Romano and Alejandro Azcárate discussed the critical insights on transitioning from SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding 1.0 to Onboarding 2.0.

The webinar highlighted the key features released in H1 2024 for SAP SuccessFactors ONB 2.0 that further improve user experience, data integration, and process efficiency.

The discussion then emphasized how the transition is not a simple "lift-and-shift", but an opportunity to rethink and optimize your onboarding processes for greater efficiency and engagement.

Key takeaways include:

  • Data Quality & Integration: The new onboarding system shares the same data model as Employee Central, ensuring seamless data quality and reducing the need for redundant mappings.
  • Enhanced UI & Mobile Experience: The new user interface is simplified, offering real-time data access and a more intuitive experience for both HR and employees, including mobile functionality for managers.
  • Efficiency Gains: Automating tasks like managing pending hires and reviewing personal data ensures faster and more accurate completion of processes, particularly helpful for industries like retail that require quick hiring cycles.

Why Now is the Right Time for Transition? SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding 1.0 set to be deprecated by 2026, starting your transition now allows you to refresh your processes while leveraging the latest onboarding features that drive productivity, employee engagement, and smoother integrations with third-party systems.

SAP SuccessFactors Services can help to support your transition, we offer a Transition Advisory and Implementation Service designed to guide you through the complexities of this upgrade. Whether you need a complete implementation or advisory support, our team is equipped with flexible service options tailored to your needs.

Ready to future-proof your onboarding process? Get in touch with our Expert today: Angela Romano angela.romano@sap.com and ensure a smooth transition to the next generation of onboarding

Additional materials:

Webinar Recording

Slides Shared during the webinar

Transition Advisory and Implementation Service Summary

Continuous Quality Check for customers currently engaged SAP Partners

entry.title: Wehre can i start learning to develop and run apps on SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Developing Applications on SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime learning journey

Overview
This Learning Journey introduces you into all important aspects to develop and run apps on SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime.

Learning objectives
Upon completing this learning journey, developers will be able to efficiently develop, run, and manage cloud-native applications on SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry Runtime. They will learn best practices for designing applications and applying advanced deployment strategies. Additionally, developers will gain skills in networking and routing, observability and monitoring, scaling, and handling updates. They will also explore advanced techniques in UI development, application sizing, security, resilience, and AI integration.

Prerequisites

Recommended:

  • Discovering SAP Business Technology Platform
  • Confident Handling of command line tools
  • General familiarity with software development and microservice patterns

Mandator:

  • Managing Clean Core for SAP S/4HANA Cloud

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit
